title: Melted Indian Cocoa Curry
description: A jammy, crunchy lazy-sunday dish that blends rich cocoa with tandoor-clay pot cooking, creating a creamy, aromatic curry.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the tandoor-clay pot over medium heat and sauté the onion until translucent.
Add the ginger and garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the ground cumin, coriander powder, and garam masala, cooking for another minute.
Pour in the coconut milk, cocoa powder, almond butter, and cane sugar, whisking until smooth.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low and let it cook for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
During the last 10 minutes of simmering, add the roasted cashews to melt slightly and thicken the curry.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt, adding more sugar if desired for jammy sweetness.
Transfer the curry to a serving dish, garnish with extra cashews, and serve hot with steamed rice or flatbread.
